title: Richly Fermented Malaysian Black Beans Fried Rice
description: A tangy and crunchy Malaysian dish featuring fermented black beans, perfect for a lazy Sunday, using a bain-marie for safe cooking temperatures and FODMAP-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat using a bain-marie for safe cooking temperatures.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Push the onion and garlic mixture to one side of the skillet, then add a little more oil if necessary and scramble in the eggs until they are cooked through.
Mix the eggs with the onion and garlic mixture.
Add the fermented black beans, cooked rice, soy sauce, salt, and pepper to the skillet, stirring constantly to combine and break up any clumps.
Cook for about 5-7 minutes, stirring frequently, until the rice is heated through and starting to brown.
Stir in the chopped scallions and cook for another minute.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the fried rice hot and enjoy.
